defs: The act of committing, doing, or performing; the act of perpetrating.
quotes: Every commission of sin introduces into the soul a certain degree of hardness.

defs: The act of intrusting; a charge; instructions as to how a trust shall be executed.
The duty or employment intrusted to any person or persons; a trust; a charge.
A formal written warrant or authority, granting certain powers or privileges and authorizing or commanding the performance of certain duties.
A certificate conferring military or naval rank and authority; as, a colonel's commission.
A company of persons joined in the performance of some duty or the execution of some trust; as, the interstate commerce commission.
quotes: A commission was at once appointed to examine into the matter.

defs: The acting under authority of, or on account of, another.
The thing to be done as agent for another; as, I have three commissions for the city.
The brokerage or allowance made to a factor or agent for transacting business for another; as, a commission of ten per cent on sales. See Del credere.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Commissioned (?); p. pr & vb. n. Commissioning.]

defs: To give a commission to; to furnish with a commission; to empower or authorize; as, to commission persons to perform certain acts; to commission an officer.
To send out with a charge or commission.
quotes: A chosen band He first commissions to the Latian land.
